title: The Undoing Project: A Friendship That Changed Our Minds
author: Michael Lewis
themes: The Friendship of Kahneman and Tversky
excerpt: The story of the unlikely friendship between two Israeli psychologists, Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky, whose collaboration revolutionized our understanding of human judgment and laid the foundations for behavioral economics—as told by one of the greatest narrative journalists of our time.
keyIdeas: Kahneman and Tversky's collaboration created the field of behavioral economics
Their work on heuristics and biases revealed systematic flaws in human judgment
The friendship between them was as remarkable as their scientific achievements
Their ideas have transformed economics, medicine, and public policy
keyTakeaways: Great science often emerges from intense human relationships
Understanding cognitive biases is the first step to overcoming them
The best thinking is often collaborative, not solitary
Scientific revolutions come from questioning fundamental assumptions
whoShouldRead: Anyone who has read Thinking, Fast and Slow and wants the backstory
Fans of Michael Lewis's narrative journalism
Students of psychology, economics, and the history of science
Anyone interested in how creative collaboration produces breakthroughs
whoShouldSkip: Those looking for practical cognitive bias advice (read Kahneman directly)
Readers who prefer science to stories about scientists
